post.title: 83% of AI deployments in regulated industries fail
post.hook: 83% of AI deployments in regulated industries fail. Most people blame the wrong thing.
post.text:
**83% of AI deployments** in regulated industries fail.
Not because the model is wrong. Because the governance isn't ready.
I call this the **Regulated AI Governance Gap**.
Most organizations build the AI. Few build the framework to run it at scale - audit trails, explainability requirements, human-in-the-loop checkpoints.
In healthcare and finance, a model that can't explain its output isn't just useless. It's a liability.
What I've seen work: treat your AI rollout like a regulated product launch. Build the governance before you build the features.
**The model is the easy part.** The accountability structure is where projects die.
What governance controls does your team have in place for AI deployment?
#AI #DigitalTransformation #Governance #HealthTech #FinTech
